included: Link Agents / Link CLI agentic payments

priorities.P2: Useful but can follow after launch if needed

epics.description: Build the hosted public booking experience customers use to view services, select appointment times, enter contact information, review policies, pay if required, and receive confirmation.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Customers can complete a booking from a hosted barber booking page.
The booking experience is optimized for mobile devices.
Customers can book without creating an account.
Customers see service price, duration, deposit requirement, and cancellation policy before confirming.
The system supports secure appointment access links after booking.

tickets.summary: Create hosted public barber booking page
tickets.description: As a customer, I want to open the barber's direct booking page so I can view the business, services, and available appointment options.
acceptanceCriteria: The public booking page is accessible through a hosted URL.
The page displays barber/business name, hero image or profile image, and basic business information.
The page displays available services and starting prices.
The page works on mobile, tablet, and desktop.
The page has clear calls to action for starting a booking.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want to select one primary service and optional add-ons so the appointment reflects what I need.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can select exactly one primary service.
Customer can select zero or more optional add-ons.
Each service displays name, description, price, and duration.
Each add-on displays name, price, and duration.
Total appointment price and duration update when add-ons are selected.

tickets.summary: Calculate total appointment price and duration
tickets.description: As a customer, I want the system to calculate the full appointment cost and duration before I book.
acceptanceCriteria: Total price includes the selected primary service and all add-ons.
Total duration includes the selected primary service and all add-ons.
The calculated duration is used when showing available appointment slots.
The calculated total is shown before the customer confirms the booking.
The system prevents booking if required pricing or duration data is missing.

tickets.summary: Build date and time selection flow
tickets.description: As a customer, I want to choose an available date and time for my selected service.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can view available dates and times.
Unavailable slots are not shown as bookable.
Available slots account for selected service duration, add-ons, buffers, blocked time, and business hours.
Customer can change selected service/add-ons and see updated availability.
Selected appointment time is shown clearly before confirmation.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want to book without creating an account by entering only required contact information.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can enter name, phone number, and email address.
Customer is not required to create an account before booking.
Phone and email fields are validated before submission.
Customer contact information is attached to the appointment record.
Optional account creation can be offered after booking confirmation.

tickets.summary: Show booking review and policy acknowledgement step
tickets.description: As a customer, I want to review appointment details, payment requirements, and policies before confirming.
acceptanceCriteria: Review step shows selected service, add-ons, date, time, total price, duration, and deposit requirement.
Review step shows cancellation and no-show policy.
Customer must explicitly acknowledge the policy before confirming.
If payment is required, the customer is directed to payment after policy acknowledgement.
If payment is not required, the appointment can confirm after policy acknowledgement.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want a clear confirmation screen after booking so I know my appointment is scheduled.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmation screen displays appointment date, time, service, duration, and location/business details.
Confirmation screen displays payment/deposit status.
Confirmation screen provides a secure appointment management link.
Confirmation screen explains how to cancel, reschedule, message, or pay remaining balance.
Customer receives confirmation notification after successful booking.

epics.description: Implement the core scheduling engine, availability rules, slot holds, appointment statuses, rescheduling, cancellations, walk-ins, blocked time, and conflict prevention.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Customers only see valid available appointment times.
The system prevents double-booking.
Appointment statuses clearly reflect the current lifecycle state.
Customers can self-reschedule once per booking.
Barber/admin can override appointment actions with audit logging.

tickets.summary: Configure business hours and booking windows
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to configure business hours and booking windows so customers only book valid times.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can configure business days and hours.
Admin can configure minimum booking notice.
Admin can configure maximum future booking window.
Configured hours are used by the customer booking calendar.
Changes to availability settings are audit logged.

tickets.summary: Configure slot intervals and buffer times
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to configure slot intervals and buffers so appointments fit the barber's workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can configure slot intervals such as 15, 30, or 60 minutes.
Admin can configure buffer time between appointments.
Availability calculations include buffer time.
Customer-facing time slots respect configured slot intervals.
Changes to slot intervals or buffers are audit logged.

tickets.summary: Support manual blocked time and closed days
tickets.description: As a barber, I want to block time for breaks, errands, vacations, or closures so customers cannot book unavailable periods.
acceptanceCriteria: Barber can create manual blocked time from the dashboard.
Admin can create closed days or vacation periods.
Blocked time is excluded from customer availability.
Blocked time can be edited or deleted by authorized users.
Blocked time changes are audit logged.

tickets.description: As a barber, I want to add walk-ins so offline appointments are represented in the schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Barber can create a walk-in appointment from the dashboard.
Walk-in appointment includes customer name, service, time, and payment status if available.
Walk-in appointment blocks the selected time from online booking.
Walk-ins appear in the calendar and appointments list.
Walk-in creation is audit logged.

tickets.description: As the system, I need to calculate valid appointment slots using services, add-ons, business hours, buffers, blocks, and existing appointments.
acceptanceCriteria: Availability engine accounts for service duration and add-on duration.
Availability engine excludes blocked time and closed days.
Availability engine excludes existing confirmed appointments.
Availability engine respects minimum notice and future booking window.
Availability engine returns only bookable slots to the customer booking flow.

tickets.summary: Implement temporary slot hold during checkout
tickets.description: As the system, I need to temporarily hold a selected appointment slot during checkout so another customer cannot confirm it at the same time.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected slot is temporarily held when customer begins final booking/payment step.
Held slot is not available to other customers during the hold window.
Default hold duration is approximately 5 minutes unless configured otherwise.
If payment or confirmation does not complete before hold expiration, the slot is released.
If two customers attempt the same slot, only the first successful confirmation/payment can secure it.

tickets.description: As the system, I need appointment statuses that accurately represent booking, payment, attendance, cancellation, and refund outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: System supports Slot Available, Slot Held, Pending Payment, Confirmed, Upcoming, Checked In, Completed, Final Payment Due, Final Payment Paid, Canceled, No-Show, Refunded, Deposit Forfeited, and No Penalty states.
Each appointment has one current primary status.
Payment-related sub-statuses are visible where relevant.
Status changes are visible in dashboard and customer appointment view where appropriate.
Sensitive status changes are audit logged.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want to cancel from my secure appointment link so I do not need to call or text.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can cancel from the secure appointment link.
Customer sees whether cancellation is inside or outside the policy window.
Customer sees whether deposit will be refunded, forfeited, or unaffected before confirming cancellation.
Canceled appointment releases the slot when applicable.
Cancellation notification is sent to customer and barber.

tickets.summary: Support one customer self-service reschedule per booking
tickets.description: As a customer, I want to reschedule once from my appointment link, while the business limits repeated rescheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can self-reschedule one time per booking.
Customer cannot self-reschedule again after the first successful self-reschedule.
Customer is informed before confirming that only one self-service reschedule is allowed.
Rescheduled appointment respects availability rules.
After self-reschedule limit is used, customer must contact barber through messaging for further changes.

tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I want to manually reschedule appointments when exceptions are approved.
acceptanceCriteria: Barber/admin can manually reschedule an appointment from the dashboard.
Manual reschedule can override customer self-reschedule limit.
Manual reschedule requires a reason note.
Manual reschedule updates customer and barber notifications.
Manual reschedule action is audit logged.

tickets.summary: Mark appointments checked in, completed, canceled, or no-show
tickets.description: As a barber, I want to update appointment attendance and completion statuses from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Barber can mark appointment as checked in.
Barber can mark appointment as completed.
Barber can mark appointment as canceled.
Barber can mark appointment as no-show.
No-show and cancellation outcomes apply configured deposit/payment policy.

epics.description: Implement Stripe/web checkout, optional deposits, remaining balance collection, payment links, in-person payment tracking, digital tipping, refunds, forfeitures, disputes, and payment exception handling.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Admin can configure whether deposits are required, optional, or disabled.
Customers can pay deposits and remaining balances online when required.
Customers can tip digitally.
Admin can issue refunds and override deposit outcomes.
Payment events are reflected in appointment status and audit logs.

tickets.summary: Integrate Stripe/web checkout for deposit payments
tickets.description: As a customer, I want to pay a required deposit online so my appointment can be confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer is sent to Stripe/web checkout when deposit payment is required.
Appointment remains Pending Payment until successful payment confirmation.
Appointment becomes Confirmed after successful required deposit payment.
Failed payment prevents appointment confirmation.
Payment result is stored on the appointment record.

tickets.summary: Configure deposit and cancellation policy rules
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to configure deposit and cancellation rules so the platform matches the business policy.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can set deposit as required, optional, or disabled.
Admin can configure service-specific deposit amount or percentage.
Admin can configure cancellation window, defaulting to 24 hours.
Admin can configure late cancellation and no-show deposit forfeiture behavior.
Policy changes are audit logged.

tickets.description: As an admin, I want bookings to confirm without payment when deposits are disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: If deposit is disabled, customer can confirm booking after contact info and policy acknowledgement.
No payment step is required when deposit is disabled.
Appointment status becomes Confirmed after successful no-deposit booking.
No-show and late cancellation financial penalties default to no penalty if no payment method exists.
No-deposit booking behavior follows admin-configured rules.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want to pay the remaining balance after the haircut using online or offline options.
acceptanceCriteria: System calculates remaining balance after deposit is applied.
Customer can pay remaining balance through web checkout.
Barber/admin can mark remaining balance as paid in person.
Payment status updates to Final Payment Paid after successful collection.
Receipt is generated after final payment.

tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I want to generate payment links for deposits, remaining balances, or tips so customers can pay from SMS/email/chat.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin/barber can generate a payment link for an appointment balance.
Payment link can be sent through chat, SMS, or email.
Payment link shows amount, appointment reference, and payment purpose.
Payment status updates after successful payment through the link.
Expired or already-paid links cannot be reused incorrectly.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want to tip digitally during or after final checkout.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can add a tip during final online checkout.
Customer can tip from a post-appointment receipt/payment link.
Tip presets include common percentage options such as 15%, 20%, and 25%.
Customer can enter a custom tip amount.
Tip amount is visible in barber/admin dashboard.

tickets.summary: Implement payment webhooks and status sync
tickets.description: As the system, I need payment provider events to update appointment and payment records reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Payment success webhook updates appointment/payment status.
Payment failure webhook updates appointment/payment status.
Refund webhook updates refund status.
Webhook handling is idempotent.
Webhook failures are logged and retried safely.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to issue refunds from the dashboard so I can handle cancellations and exceptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can issue full or partial refund from the dashboard.
Refund action requires confirmation.
Refund action requires or supports a reason note.
Refund status is visible on the appointment/payment record.
Refund action is audit logged.

tickets.summary: Support deposit refund and forfeiture overrides
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to manually override deposit refund or forfeiture outcomes when exceptions occur.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can override deposit outcome to refunded, forfeited, or no penalty.
Override requires a reason note.
Override updates appointment payment status.
Customer notification is sent if configured.
Override is audit logged.

tickets.summary: Show disputes, chargebacks, and failed payments
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want visibility into disputes, chargebacks, and failed payments so I can manage payment risk.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays failed payment records.
Dashboard displays dispute or chargeback status when available.
Admin can view appointment/customer/payment context for payment exceptions.
Admin can add internal notes to payment exceptions.
Payment exception notes are retained with audit history.

epics.description: Support customer-side agentic booking and payment using Link Agents, including structured booking metadata, agent-initiated booking, programmatic payment, virtual-card fallback, and payment approval handling.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Agents can read structured booking information without scraping the UI.
Agents can initiate booking/payment flows.
Customer approval is required before agent-initiated payment succeeds.
Programmatic payment is preferred where supported.
Virtual-card checkout fallback is supported.

tickets.description: As a customer-side agent, I need structured booking data so I can understand services, prices, policies, and availability.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema includes services, add-ons, prices, durations, deposits, policies, and available slots.
Schema includes booking action endpoints or instructions.
Schema is versioned.
Schema avoids exposing unnecessary customer or admin data.
Schema can be consumed by an agent without relying on UI scraping.

tickets.description: As a customer-side agent, I want to retrieve structured booking data from the booking page.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint returns current services, add-ons, prices, durations, policies, and availability.
Endpoint response follows the approved metadata schema.
Endpoint does not expose private dashboard data.
Endpoint handles unavailable or outdated slot data safely.
Endpoint response includes schema version.

tickets.description: As a customer-side agent, I want to initiate a booking request for a selected service, time, and customer.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent can submit selected service, add-ons, appointment time, and customer contact details.
System creates a temporary slot hold for valid agent-initiated booking requests.
System rejects invalid, unavailable, or expired slot requests.
System returns payment requirements and next action.
Agent-initiated booking does not bypass customer approval or policy rules.

tickets.description: As a customer-side agent, I want to complete payment through a programmatic Link-supported flow when available.
acceptanceCriteria: System can create or process a Link-compatible payment request for an agent-initiated booking.
Customer approval is required before payment succeeds.
Payment result updates appointment status.
Programmatic payment failure prevents payment-required booking confirmation.
Payment flow does not expose raw payment credentials to the application.

tickets.description: As a customer-side agent, I want to use a virtual-card fallback when programmatic payment is not supported.
acceptanceCriteria: Agentic flow can fall back to normal checkout when programmatic payment is unavailable.
Virtual-card payment follows standard checkout payment rules.
Appointment confirms only after successful payment when payment is required.
Failed virtual-card payment releases or expires the slot hold according to policy.
Fallback path is recorded as the payment method/source.

tickets.summary: Handle delayed, declined, or expired Link approval
tickets.description: As the system, I need to handle pending Link approvals so appointment inventory is not blocked indefinitely.
acceptanceCriteria: If Link approval is pending, appointment remains unconfirmed.
Slot remains held only for the configured hold duration.
If approval is declined, appointment is not confirmed and slot is released.
If approval does not complete before hold expiration, slot is released.
Customer and agent receive clear failure or expiration state.

tickets.summary: Log and report Link payment outcomes
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want visibility into Link payment attempts, approvals, declines, and expirations.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows Link payment status on relevant appointment records.
System logs Link approval delays, declines, expirations, and successful payments.
Link payment attempts are included in payment reporting.
Admin can filter payment records by Link payment source.
Link payment failures are included in payment failure alerts.

epics.description: Build the secure dashboard used by the barber and owner/admin to manage calendar, appointments, clients, messages, payments, settings, policies, reports, and operational exceptions.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Barber can manage daily operations from the dashboard.
Owner/admin can configure services, prices, availability, policies, and payment settings.
Dashboard provides visibility into appointments, payments, messages, and reports.
Sensitive dashboard actions are permission-controlled.
Operational changes are audit logged.

tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I want a dashboard with clear navigation so I can access calendar, appointments, clients, messages, payments, settings, and reports.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard includes navigation for Calendar, Appointments, Clients, Messages, Payments, Services, Availability, Policies, Notifications, Reports, and Settings.
Navigation is usable on desktop and tablet.
Current section is visually clear.
User only sees sections allowed by their role.
Dashboard shell supports authenticated sessions.

tickets.description: As a barber, I want a calendar view so I can see my daily and upcoming schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar displays confirmed, upcoming, completed, canceled, and no-show appointments.
Calendar displays blocked time and walk-ins.
Calendar supports day and week views.
Calendar items show appointment time, customer name, service, and status.
Barber can open appointment details from the calendar.

tickets.summary: Build appointments list and detail view
tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I want a searchable appointments list and detail page so I can manage appointment operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Appointments list supports filtering by date, status, payment status, and customer.
Appointment detail shows service, add-ons, time, customer contact, messages, payment status, and policy status.
Barber/admin can update eligible appointment statuses.
Appointment detail shows secure customer appointment link.
Sensitive changes are audit logged.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to view payments, deposits, tips, refunds, failed payments, and balances from one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays deposits, remaining balances, tips, refunds, and failed payments.
Payment records can be filtered by date, status, method, and appointment.
Payment records link back to appointment details.
Admin can see whether a payment was Stripe, Link, payment link, or in-person.
Payment dashboard supports refund and exception workflows where permitted.

tickets.summary: Build client list and client history
tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I want to view client history so I can understand repeat visits and appointment context.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard includes client list with name, phone, email, and latest appointment.
Client detail shows appointment history.
Client detail shows payment and no-show/cancellation history where permitted.
Client detail links to related appointment messages.
Client records are created from guest bookings and walk-ins.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to manage services, add-ons, prices, durations, and deposits.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create, edit, disable, and reorder services.
Admin can create, edit, disable, and reorder add-ons.
Admin can set price and duration for services and add-ons.
Admin can set service-specific deposit rules.
Service/pricing changes are audit logged.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to manage business hours, slot intervals, buffers, blocked days, and booking windows.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can edit business hours.
Admin can edit slot interval and buffer time.
Admin can edit minimum booking notice and maximum future booking window.
Admin can create vacation or closed days.
Availability changes are audit logged and reflected in customer booking flow.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want lightweight reports so I can understand bookings, revenue, cancellations, no-shows, tips, and payment performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports show appointment volume, completed bookings, cancellations, no-shows, and reschedules.
Reports show deposits collected, refunds, forfeitures, tips, and failed payments.
Reports can be filtered by date range.
Reports include service popularity.
Reports are visible only to authorized owner/admin users.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to configure notification behavior for confirmations, reminders, receipts, cancellations, reschedules, and messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can configure reminder timing.
Admin can enable or disable email reminder types where allowed.
Admin can enable or disable SMS reminder types where allowed.
Admin can preview notification templates or content summaries.
Notification setting changes are audit logged.

epics.summary: Authentication, Roles, Security, Privacy, and Auditability
epics.description: Implement secure access, customer appointment links, barber/admin authentication, role-based permissions, MFA, audit logs, encryption, privacy controls, and deletion request handling.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Owner/admin and barber users authenticate securely.
Owner/admin accounts support MFA.
Customers access appointment management through secure links.
Roles control access to dashboard capabilities.
Sensitive actions are audit logged.

tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I need secure login so only authorized users can access the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Barber/admin users can log in securely.
Invalid login attempts are rejected.
Sessions expire according to configured security rules.
Password reset or account recovery flow is available.
Dashboard routes require authentication.

tickets.description: As the system, I need role-based permissions so customer, agent, barber, and owner/admin capabilities are properly separated.
acceptanceCriteria: System supports Customer/Guest, Customer-Side Agent, Barber, and Owner/Admin roles.
Barber can manage day-to-day appointment operations.
Owner/Admin can manage services, policies, payments, settings, refunds, and reports.
Unauthorized users cannot access restricted dashboard modules.
Permission checks are enforced server-side.

tickets.summary: Implement audit logging for sensitive actions
tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want sensitive changes to be auditable so operational and payment decisions can be traced.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit logs capture actor, timestamp, action, old value, new value, and reason note where applicable.
Appointment status changes are audit logged.
Payment refunds, forfeitures, and overrides are audit logged.
Policy, service, pricing, and availability changes are audit logged.
Audit logs cannot be deleted by standard dashboard users.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I need MFA to protect sensitive payment and policy settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Owner/admin accounts can enroll in MFA.
Owner/admin login requires MFA after enrollment.
Recovery flow exists for lost MFA access.
Barber-only role can have MFA optional unless elevated permissions are granted.
MFA events are logged.

tickets.summary: Create secure customer appointment access links
tickets.description: As a customer, I want to access my appointment without an account while keeping my appointment private.
acceptanceCriteria: Each appointment has a secure, hard-to-guess management link.
Secure link allows access only to that appointment's permitted customer actions.
Secure link cannot expose other customer or dashboard data.
Secure link access can be expired or revoked by admin if needed.
Sensitive actions from secure links are logged.

tickets.summary: Implement sensitive data protection and encryption requirements
tickets.description: As the system, I need to protect customer, appointment, message, and payment-related data.
acceptanceCriteria: Sensitive data is encrypted in transit.
Sensitive stored data is protected according to production security standards.
Raw payment credentials are never stored by the application.
Payment methods are tokenized through payment providers.
Access to sensitive data is restricted by role.

tickets.summary: Implement privacy controls and deletion request handling
tickets.description: As a customer, I want basic privacy protections and a way to request deletion of my data.
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy policy is linked from public booking page.
Customer consents to required SMS/email communications during booking.
System supports internal handling of customer data deletion requests.
Deletion handling preserves legally or operationally required payment/audit records where needed.
Privacy-related actions are logged.

epics.description: Implement full appointment-linked chat, SMS/email relay, booking lifecycle notifications, reminders, receipts, refund notifications, and notification delivery logging.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Customers can message the barber without creating an account.
Barber can manage appointment-linked messages from dashboard.
SMS and email notifications support booking lifecycle events.
Notification delivery failures are logged.
Messaging history is preserved with appointments.

tickets.description: As the system, I need message threads tied to appointments so customer/barber communication stays contextual.
acceptanceCriteria: Each appointment can have one message thread.
Messages store sender, timestamp, content, and delivery status where available.
Message history is retained with the appointment.
Message access follows customer secure link and dashboard role permissions.
Message data is protected as sensitive customer communication.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want to message the barber from my secure appointment link.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer can open appointment chat from secure appointment link.
Customer can send messages without creating an account.
Customer can view appointment-specific message history.
Customer receives clear success or failure feedback after sending a message.
Customer cannot access unrelated message threads.

tickets.description: As a barber, I want to view and reply to appointment messages from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Barber can view all appointment-linked conversations.
Barber can reply to customer messages.
Messages are linked to appointment detail records.
Unread or new messages are visually indicated.
Message replies can trigger customer SMS/email relay notifications.

tickets.description: As the system, I need SMS delivery for confirmations, reminders, messages, cancellations, reschedules, and payment links.
acceptanceCriteria: System can send SMS confirmation after booking.
System can send SMS appointment reminders.
System can send SMS alerts for messages.
System can send SMS cancellation and reschedule confirmations.
SMS delivery status or failure is logged when provider supports it.

tickets.description: As the system, I need email delivery for confirmations, reminders, receipts, cancellations, reschedules, refunds, and messages.
acceptanceCriteria: System can send booking confirmation email.
System can send appointment reminder email when enabled.
System can send payment receipt email.
System can send cancellation, reschedule, refund, and deposit forfeiture emails.
Email delivery failures are logged when provider supports it.

tickets.description: As a customer and barber, I want timely notifications when bookings, cancellations, reschedules, payments, refunds, or messages happen.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmation notification is sent after appointment confirmation.
Reminder notifications are sent based on configured timing.
Cancellation notifications are sent after cancellation.
Reschedule notifications are sent after reschedule.
Payment receipt, refund, and deposit forfeiture notifications are sent when applicable.

tickets.description: As an admin, I want failed notifications to be logged so communication issues can be investigated.
acceptanceCriteria: Failed SMS sends are logged.
Failed email sends are logged.
Notification logs include recipient, event type, timestamp, and provider status when available.
Critical notification failures are visible to admin or monitoring.
Notification failures do not corrupt appointment status.

epics.description: Implement CSV exports, external calendar sync, and supporting integrations needed for production v1 operations.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Owner/admin can export appointment and payment records.
Dashboard can sync appointments to an external calendar.
The product does not require accounting integrations for v1.
Integration failures are logged.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to export appointment records so I can review or reconcile operations externally.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can export appointments as CSV.
Export supports date range filtering.
Export includes customer, service, appointment time, status, payment status, and created date.
Export respects role permissions.
Export action is logged.

tickets.description: As an owner/admin, I want to export payment records so I can reconcile deposits, balances, refunds, and tips.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can export payments as CSV.
Export supports date range and payment status filtering.
Export includes deposit, remaining balance, tip, refund, forfeiture, payment method/source, and appointment reference.
Export respects role permissions.
Export action is logged.

tickets.description: As a barber, I want appointments synced to an external calendar so I can view my schedule outside the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmed appointments can sync to external calendar.
Canceled or rescheduled appointments update external calendar entries.
Dashboard remains the source of truth for booking availability.
Calendar sync errors are logged.
Admin/barber can enable or disable calendar sync.

epics.description: Implement production monitoring, failure alerts, metrics tracking, webhook reliability, booking/payment observability, and safeguards against double-booking.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Critical booking and payment failures are monitored.
Webhook processing is reliable and idempotent.
System tracks key booking, payment, notification, and messaging metrics.
Double-booking protections are tested.
Admins can be alerted to critical failures.

tickets.description: As the operations team, I need error monitoring so production issues can be detected quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend errors are captured.
Backend errors are captured.
Payment and booking errors are tagged for filtering.
Critical errors trigger alerts.
Monitoring avoids logging sensitive payment credentials.

tickets.summary: Track booking and payment funnel metrics
tickets.description: As product/admin, I want metrics for booking and payment performance so v1 success can be measured.
acceptanceCriteria: System tracks booking page visits, booking starts, and booking completions.
System tracks booking abandonment.
System tracks payment success and failure rates.
System tracks Link approval delays, declines, and expirations.
System tracks no-shows, cancellations, reschedules, refunds, and forfeitures.

tickets.summary: Create booking and payment failure alerts
tickets.description: As an admin/operator, I want alerts for critical booking and payment failures so issues can be resolved quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Critical booking confirmation failures trigger alerts.
Payment webhook failures trigger alerts.
Repeated failed payments or checkout errors trigger alerts.
Link payment approval expiration spikes can be detected.
Alerts include enough context for investigation without exposing sensitive credentials.

tickets.summary: Implement idempotency and retry handling for payment webhooks
tickets.description: As the system, I need payment webhook processing to be reliable and safe from duplicate events.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate webhook events do not create duplicate payments or refunds.
Webhook processing can safely retry failed events.
Webhook failures are recorded.
Payment state transitions remain consistent after retries.
Webhook processing includes tests for duplicate and out-of-order events.

tickets.description: As the business, I need confidence that two customers cannot book the same slot.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests cover simultaneous booking attempts for the same slot.
Automated tests cover expired slot holds.
Automated tests cover successful payment after slot hold.
Automated tests cover payment failure and slot release.
Manual QA verifies first successful payment/confirmation wins.

epics.description: Apply the uploaded concept screens as screen/layout direction, build polished customer and dashboard UI, ensure responsive behavior, and meet accessibility expectations.
epicAcceptanceCriteria: Public booking website is polished and mobile-first.
Dashboard is usable on desktop and tablet.
Concept images guide required screens and layout direction.
Product targets WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility.
Important flows are clear, readable, and easy to complete.

tickets.summary: Translate concept images into screen requirements
tickets.description: As the product team, I want the concept images translated into required screens and components while allowing final styling to evolve.
acceptanceCriteria: Required customer booking screens are identified.
Required dashboard screens are identified.
Layout direction from concept images is documented.
Exact styling is not treated as locked unless separately approved.
Design requirements map back to PRD scope.

tickets.description: As a customer, I want the booking experience to work smoothly on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: Booking page layout is optimized for mobile screens.
Tap targets are large enough for mobile use.
Service, time, policy, and payment steps are readable on small screens.
Booking flow works on common mobile browsers.
Desktop and tablet layouts remain usable.

tickets.description: As a barber/admin, I want the dashboard to work well on desktop and tablet.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard is optimized for desktop use.
Dashboard is usable on tablet.
Calendar, appointments, messages, and payments views remain readable.
Navigation adapts to available screen size.
Critical appointment actions remain easy to access.

tickets.description: As a customer or dashboard user, I want the product to be accessible and usable with assistive technologies.
acceptanceCriteria: Public booking forms have labels and accessible error states.
Dashboard navigation supports keyboard navigation.
Color contrast targets WCAG 2.2 AA.
Important UI states are not communicated by color alone.
Screen-reader-friendly labels are added to interactive controls.

criteria: Customer can complete a guest booking from the hosted public page.
Booking respects availability, service duration, add-ons, buffers, and blocked time.
Customer can cancel and self-reschedule once.
Payment webhooks are reliable and idempotent.
Programmatic Link payment path works where supported.
Delayed or declined Link approval releases held slot correctly.
Barber can view and manage calendar.
Barber can add walk-ins and blocked time.
Owner/admin can manage services, pricing, availability, policies, and notification settings.
Payments, refunds, tips, and failed payments are visible.
